description: LTX-2.3 22B HDR enhancement via the IC-LoRA 'LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-HDR'. Video-to-video: feed an SDR clip, get an HDR-graded look. Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_ICLoRA_HDR_Distilled' (cloud GemmaAPITextEncode bypassed → local encoder path). This is a TIER-2 / IC-LoRA preset: the IC-LoRA rides a model patch (LTXICLoRALoaderModelOnly) AND a guide-conditioning path (LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuide), so unlike Tier-1 catalog LoRAs it cannot be free-stacked — it ships as its own preset.

about.what: Takes an ordinary (SDR) video and re-grades it into an HDR look — brighter highlights and a wider range of light — while keeping the original motion and content.
about.key_knobs: Prompt — keep it tiny (the shipped example is literally 'HDR footage'); the look comes from the video itself, not a long description.

_TEMPLATE: READY-TO-RUN. The TWO shared LTX-2.3 base weights (dev checkpoint, gemma text encoder) are pinned to real URLs — the SAME two as ltx2_generate, now verified/pinned. The distill LoRA is now PINNED to the cond-safe rank-72 variant (not the official rank-384) and the HDR IC-LoRA URL itself is REAL/verified. Run --convert-only FIRST (skips the weight pull) to confirm the graph converts and to discover any missing node_packs from the validation errors, then fill weights and render.
_HOWITRUNS: Same no-build path as the other presets: public base image, comfy_run.py clones node_packs, downloads weights, converts the UI graph to API, applies patches, optionally splices a Tier-1 --lora stack onto the checkpoint (anchor 3940), and renders.
_CONSTRAINTS: Output dims should give an even latent (each divisible by 64) for the IC-LoRA guide. HDR output is written as float16 EXR frames (LTXVHDRDecodePostprocess save_exr) — comfy_run sets OPENCV_IO_ENABLE_OPENEXR=1 so OpenCV can write them; a render yields a sequence of frame_#####.exr (plus an mp4 preview). The HDR IC-LoRA weight is gated (Lightricks/LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-HDR) — accept its HF license for the token's account or the weight download 403s.
_BATCH: Batch-ready: pass multiple SDR clips (and/or EXR/PNG/TIFF frame folders) with --batch / the web Batch picker to re-grade them all in one job (the model loads once). input_anchor (GetVideoComponents 5105) lets a frame-sequence input swap in; output_prefix names the mp4 preview AND gives each item its own HDR EXR folder (output/<stem>_hdr_exr) so a batch never overwrites itself.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 HDR — convert an SDR clip to an HDR look (IC-LoRA)

primary_input.label: Input video (SDR)
primary_input.tooltip: The clip to re-grade; output resolution + fps follow the INPUT (v2v). LTX-2.3 supports up to 4K/UHD @ up to 50fps for clips <=10s (10-20s caps at 25fps/1080p); dims should be divisible by 32. The HDR IC-LoRA enhances dynamic range / color from the surrounding video.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Keep it SHORT — Lightricks' shipped example for this graph is literally 'HDR footage'. Add a brief scene descriptor only if needed; the IC-LoRA derives the look from the input video, not a long prompt. (src: LTX-2.3 IC-LoRA HDR example workflow)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, ugly, still, static, slow

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Lightricks' shipped HDR negative — note it adds 'still, static, slow' (vs the base negative) to keep motion alive. (src: HDR example workflow)
